title: Mitigation Coverage
text:
This chart shows which what defensive controls would reduce the effectiveness of the techniques Volt Typhoon is known to use. For example, ATT&CK identifies Privileged Account Management as a mitigation for 39 documented Volt Typhoon technique relationships.


So What? - The numbers represent breadth of defensive coverage, not strength of protection. Higher values indicate controls that are relevant to a larger share of Volt Typhoon’s documented tradecraft.  - The mitigations most frequently associated with Volt Typhoon techniques focus on account security, auditing, and privilege management. - This suggests that the most effective defense against Volt Typhoon is not traditional malware detection, but rather strong identity security, continuous monitoring, and limiting adversaries’ ability to abuse legitimate credentials.

title: Detection Sources
text: This chart shows the ATT&CK detection data components most frequently associated with techniques attributed to Volt Typhoon. Each bar represents the number of ATT&CK detection references that rely on a specific telemetry source, such as process creation events, command execution logs, network traffic content, or user authentication records. Higher values indicate that a particular data source is relevant to detecting a larger portion of Volt Typhoon’s documented techniques.  So what? - Volt Typhoon frequently uses legitimate administrative tools and valid credentials, making behavior-based monitoring more valuable than traditional antivirus signatures. - The telemetry most useful for detecting Volt Typhoon is generated by normal user and system activity, i.e.  process creation  ,  command execution  ,  authentication events, and  network communications. - Effective defense against Volt Typhoon depends less on signature-based malware detection and more on maintaining comprehensive visibility into endpoint, authentication, and network behavior.

title: Volt Typhoon Analysis
text: This website presents an overview of Volt Typhoon activity, mapped to MITRE ATT&CK tactics, detection sources, and mitigations.
